officialSources.title: Collaborate in Sheets
evidence: Google documents sharing Sheets with Viewer, Commenter, or Editor access.
Google documents comments, task assignment, email collaborators, Activity dashboard, and protected ranges in the Sheets collaboration help page.

officialSources.title: Share files from Google Drive
evidence: Google documents role-based file sharing for Viewer, Commenter, and Editor access.
Google documents share-link and restricted access controls for Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides.

officialSources.title: Share and collaborate on a spreadsheet
evidence: Google documents simultaneous collaboration limits for Docs, Sheets and Slides.
Google documents that when 100 or more users access a file, only the owner and some users with edit permissions can edit.

officialSources.title: View the activity on your Google Docs, Sheets and Slides
evidence: Google documents an Activity dashboard for Docs, Sheets and Slides.
Google documents viewer and activity settings that can affect visible collaboration data.

officialSources.title: Collaborate on Excel workbooks at the same time with co-authoring
evidence: Microsoft documents Excel co-authoring requirements, supported versions, OneDrive or SharePoint storage, and AutoSave dependency.
Microsoft documents seeing other coauthors' names and colored selections while editing supported cloud workbooks.

officialSources.title: Quick tips: Share and collaborate with Excel for Windows
evidence: Microsoft documents saving a workbook to OneDrive to access and collaborate from different devices.
Microsoft documents sharing with view or edit permissions.

officialSources.title: Insert comments and notes in Excel
evidence: Microsoft documents threaded comments, notes, and a Comments task pane in Excel.
Microsoft documents @mentions in comments for email notification and task assignment.

officialSources.title: Use @mention in comments to tag someone for feedback
evidence: Microsoft documents @mentions in Word, Excel and PowerPoint comments.
Microsoft documents assigning tasks to users from comment replies.

limitations: This artifact captures current official public documentation, not a live multi-user browser benchmark against Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel.
The comparison proves direct external collaboration evidence exists; it does not claim bilig is 10x better across collaboration or every objective category.

dimensions.title: Simultaneous Editing And Presence
biligEvidence: The collaboration scorecard verifies presence session lifecycle, selection schema validation, collaborator filtering, and headed multi-user viewport soak coverage.
The large-workbook SLO scorecard separately covers headed browser frame budgets while interacting with workbook viewports.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents real-time spreadsheet collaboration with role-based access, comments, activity dashboard, and user access limits.
Google documents that high-concurrency files shift editing behavior once 100 or more users access a file.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents Excel co-authoring with supported Excel versions, OneDrive or SharePoint storage, AutoSave, coauthor names, and colored selections.
Microsoft documents that all coauthors need AutoSave enabled for colored selections in supported Microsoft 365 scenarios.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig has executable presence and viewport-soak controls; Sheets and Excel have documented cloud collaboration with incumbent-specific storage, account, version, and concurrency requirements.
residualLimitations: bilig still needs a direct multi-user hosted browser benchmark against Sheets and Excel before claiming collaboration dominance.
The external comparison is documentation-backed, not a tenant-specific live run.

dimensions.title: Sharing Permissions And Access
biligEvidence: The current collaboration scorecard focuses on sync, presence, conflict, and viewport behavior.
Security and auditability scorecards separately cover shared-agent owner review and change-review rails.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents Viewer, Commenter, and Editor roles plus restricted and link-sharing access.
Google documents protected sheets and ranges for constraining editor updates.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents sharing Excel workbooks from OneDrive with view or edit permissions.
Microsoft documents cloud storage and supported clients as co-authoring prerequisites.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig has collaboration mechanics checked, while Google Sheets and Excel have broader documented sharing and permission surfaces.
residualLimitations: bilig needs generated role/share-permission evidence before this dimension can become a product-wide collaboration lead claim.
Tenant and storage policies can materially change incumbent access behavior.

dimensions.title: Comments Mentions And Tasks
biligEvidence: bilig's checked collaboration evidence covers workbook presence, sync, conflict, and viewport correctness.
bilig does not yet have generated comment-thread, mention, or task-assignment collaboration evidence.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents comments, task assignment, and email collaborators from Sheets.
Google documents Activity dashboard support for Docs, Sheets and Slides.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents threaded comments, notes, @mentions, task assignment, and email notifications in Excel and Office.
Microsoft documents a Comments task pane for navigating worksheet comments.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig has strong workbook-operation collaboration controls, while Sheets and Excel have broader documented comment and task collaboration surfaces.
residualLimitations: This remains a collaboration product gap for overall dominance.
Future bilig work should add comment/thread/task workflows or explicitly scope collaboration away from annotation workflows.

dimensions.title: Collaboration Limits And Conflict Recovery
biligEvidence: The collaboration scorecard verifies accepted-operation convergence and zero unexpected conflicts across a long-running collaboration control.
The editor conflict and viewport control verifies authoritative drift detection, optimistic axis protection, and authoritative catchup behavior.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents simultaneous access limits and degraded edit availability above documented collaboration thresholds.
Google documents role and access controls that affect who can edit during collaboration.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents Excel co-authoring dependencies on supported clients, OneDrive or SharePoint, and AutoSave.
Microsoft documents that unsupported versions or locations affect live co-authoring availability.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig has executable long-running conflict-rate evidence; Sheets and Excel publish collaboration limits and prerequisites instead of repo-executable conflict contracts.
residualLimitations: bilig still needs adversarial multi-user conflict tests against larger workbook scenarios.
The comparison does not measure real incumbent conflict frequency under identical edit streams.
